summary: Backend-only testing for the new in-app messaging feature (iter41). Wrote pytest suite covering all 14 test scenarios from the review request, seeded 2 IT41_ companies + 2 company_admins + 4 employees + 1 super_admin + 1 extra-admin (in-company negative case) directly via pymongo, and cleaned up on teardown. 21/21 tests PASSED. All scope, RBAC, broadcast, read-idempotency, and recipient-list contracts behave as specified.

minor.issue: For super_admin with broadcast=true and empty string company_id, code correctly normalises to None (line 4348). Just documenting behaviour — not a bug.

Response includes full recipient_user_ids array of every employee id who received the message. Spec requires recipient_count + read_count (both present). Consider whether leaking recipient_user_ids from admin outbox is intentional — currently exposed.

action_items: None — messaging backend passes 100% of specified contract tests. Main agent can proceed with frontend work.

Optional: consider whether GET /api/messages/sent should redact recipient_user_ids (only leaving recipient_count/read_count) if admins don't need the raw list. Currently exposed but not covered by the spec.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py lines 4323-4397 (send_message): logic is clean; scope filtering via mongo $in query correctly enforces company boundaries for company_admin and honours super_admin cross-company or scoped broadcast.
server.py line 4425: /inbox strips read_by per-item — confirmed no leakage.
server.py line 4470: $addToSet ensures idempotent mark-read — confirmed via assertion that read_by contains user exactly once after 2 calls.
server.py line 4488: /recipients ignores ?company_id when caller is company_admin — confirmed.
server.py file size is 5780 lines — well above the 700-line guideline; recommend splitting into routers (auth/messages/leaves/etc.) in a follow-up refactor (not blocking).
seed_data_creation: Seeded via pymongo inside module-scoped fixture: 2 companies (IT41_COMP_A, IT41_COMP_B) with unique company_code; 4 employees (IT41_user_empA1/A2/B1/B2), 2 company_admins (IT41_user_adminA/B), 1 extra company_admin in company A (IT41_user_adminA2 — used to prove non-employee admins never receive broadcasts), 1 throwaway super_admin (IT41_user_super_*). Direct session inserts (IT41_tok_*) avoid touching real super_admin PIN. Full teardown deletes every IT41_ prefixed doc across companies/users/user_sessions/messages.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Suite is idempotent and self-cleaning. Uses EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL (Expo convention) not EXPO_BACKEND_URL. Reuse test_messaging_iter41.py as regression when new admin-messaging endpoints (attachments, DM replies, per-user read receipts) are added. Skip touching db.users doc for sksharmaconsultancy@gmail.com — this suite already avoids it.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bugs. Only setup wrinkle encountered: db.companies has a unique index on company_code (null), which required seeding both test companies with distinct company_code values. Fixed in seed fixture.
